prompt: Give me a current read on Vermillion Rise Warehouse.

observations: tools fired: streamingToolPolicy, searchTeamsMessages, searchEmails, searchMeetingsByTopic, getRFIStatus, getScheduleAnalysis, getSubmittalStatus, getProjectBudgetSummary, getRFIStatus, getSubmittalStatus, getDirectCostsSummary, getCommitmentsOverview, getChangeOrderDetails, getCommitmentsOverview, getProjectBriefingSnapshot, getChangeOrderDetails, getForecastComparison, getMarginAnalysis, getProjectBriefingSnapshot, getProjectRiskAnalysis, getActionItemsAndInsights, getActionItemsAndInsights, searchTeamsMessages, searchEmails, searchEmails, searchEmails, semanticSearch, searchMeetingsByTopic, searchDocuments, searchMeetingsByTopic, findProject, findProject, getProjectDetails, noToolRetry
score.finalText:
Here’s the straight read on Vermillion Rise Warehouse as of today:

### Hard Facts
- **Budget:** $12,940,266 (variance: -$12,865,266 — well under budget; track if this reflects incomplete/lower scope or missed exposure)
- **Contract Value:** $75,000 held (double-check: contract value is notably out of line with budget—possible phase/milestone structuring or missing update)
- **Recent Change Orders:** 3 approved totaling $5,000; $1,235 pending
- **Open Change Events:** 7 remain, some unpriced
- **Open RFIs:** 4 on the books
- **Incomplete Schedule Tasks:** 41 still open — red flag for near-term closeout or turnover
- **Recent Payments:** Still seeing lag; no confirmed paid invoices
- **Recent OAC and Meetings:** Focus has shifted to closeout, change order signoff, and owner communication on LOI/warranty language
- **Safety:** Controlled, but welding/relocation and fire system integration flagged last meeting

### What’s Changed Lately
- Meetings have pivoted from design/field to commercial/closeout coordination
- Electrical rough-in delays are a current friction point; RFI drafted for clarity on impact and cost responsibility
- Change order signature tracking has become critical as cost/scope alignment tightens at the finish
- Owner wants line of sight on final costs, sequence, and warranty wrap-up

### Insider Analysis
The under-budget position looks solid on the surface, but it reflects both low spend and unpriced scope changes, not true cost avoidance. The number of open change events and incomplete schedule tasks points to a squeeze between scope, budget, and owner expectations as closeout pushes. The contract vs. budget delta suggests that either not all commitments have been pushed into the system, or the contract phasing doesn't match overall spend; clarify this before representing final savings to the owner.

RFIs are not out of control but are slow-moving — that’s a warning sign if transition to punchlist/final is coming up. Payments are lagging; don’t let that stall subs in the last mile.

### Recommended Actions
1. **Get all open change events (7) costed with ROMs by the next OAC.** Owner needs realistic exposure for final buy-in.
2. **Close gap between contract value and budget in records.** If this is phasing, document it; if not, true up the commitment log and flag any delayed buyouts.
3. **Push to get 41 open schedule items cleared or reforecasted—today.** Assign owners. If they threaten TCO, escalate.
4. **RFI on electrical needs to pinpoint whether delay costs land with you or the trade.** Owner wants to see aggressive risk management right now.
5. **Follow up on payment release for any critical subs still outstanding.** Don’t let cash flow become a closeout Achilles heel.

### Confidence/Data Gaps
- Contract vs. budget discrepancy is still unclear. Check most recent meeting and financial record for a reconciliation.
- Some cost exposure sits in unpriced/unapproved change events; until those are nailed down, variance is not true savings.
- Schedule risk is real if 41 tasks aren’t closed or rescheduled with new logic.

### Next Step
Escalate RFI follow-up and get a short list — with dollar values and assigned owners — of every unresolved change event and incomplete schedule task, before your next client-facing status call.

If you need line-item detail, cash flow, or a roll-up for owner update, say the word and I’ll pull it.

results.title: Email: Re: 2026-018 Vermillion Rise TI
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the Vermillion Rise TI project, specifically referenced as 2026-018. The email chain involves multiple parties, including Bob Wright, Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group, and Jerome Daksiewicz, Director of Design at DKGR. The purpose of the communication is to confirm the sharing of project files and access to design resources among team members involved in the project.

Key topics discussed in the email include the sharing of base plans received from CAD files and ensuring that all relevant disciplines, particularly the Mechanical team, have access to necessary project documents. Bob Wright requests confirmation from Noah Porter, an Architectural Graduate, that the plans have been distributed to all involved parties, including Clay Robinson from Enviro-Max. Jerome Daksiewicz confirms that Clay has been added to the Bim360 project for access to the Revit model and will receive the PDF file for reference.

Important details include the date of the emails, which are dated April 2 and April 3, 2026. Contact information for the involved parties is also provided, including phone numbers and addresses for their respective offices in St. Petersburg, FL, and Indianapolis, IN. The action items include confirming the distribution of plans to all disciplines and ensuring that Clay Robinson has the necessary access to the project files. The communication emphasizes collaboration and the importance of keeping all team members informed and equipped with the right resources for the project.
results.citation: Email from ["Bob  Wright"], Apr 2, 2026: "Email: Re: 2026-018 Vermillion Rise TI"

results.title: Email: RE: TAG 11/6/25 Vermillion Rise - Code Review
results.content:
The document is an email thread regarding the code review for the Vermillion Rise project, dated January 22, 2026. It involves multiple stakeholders from the Alleato Group, including Andrew Harpenau, AJ Taylor, and Brandon Clymer, discussing equipment specifications, pricing, and project updates. The primary purpose of this correspondence is to clarify details related to the project's equipment needs and costs, particularly in light of future expansions in the TCCA area.

Key topics discussed include the equipment numbers and quotes from Enviro-Max, with specific attention to a previously mentioned figure of $895,000, which AJ Taylor inquires about to confirm whether it includes future expansion considerations. The email chain also references the need for updated architectural plans following a board decision to reduce the building size by half. The communication highlights the urgency of obtaining updated equipment information to share with electricians for pricing purposes.

Important details include the confirmation of three units required for the project—two for production and one for the remainder area. The email thread indicates that updated pricing and equipment selections were to be finalized shortly, with a request for a formal order revision to be sent to Aaon. Action items include ensuring that all stakeholders have the necessary equipment information and confirming the final project approvals and architectural updates, which are expected to be received by the end of the week.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Harpenau"], Jan 21, 2026: "Email: RE: TAG 11/6/25 Vermillion Rise - Code Review"

results.title: Email: Re: Vermillion Rise TI
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the Vermillion Rise Tenant Improvement (TI) project, primarily involving discussions about design and layout changes. The email is dated April 8, 2026, and is sent by Brandon Clymer, CEO of Alleato Group, to Jerome Daksiewicz and Bob Wright. The communication reflects ongoing project management and design considerations as the team works on the specifications for the tenant improvements.

Key topics discussed in the email include the addition of a vestibule at the main entrance next to Conference Room 37, the potential installation of a restroom for the office at IT 40, and the relocation of the IT closet to the upstairs area. Jerome Daksiewicz also raises questions about the need for a wellness room, which was included in a previous design version, and the access to the breakroom from the corridor rather than from the work floor. Additionally, there is a mention of a second corridor to the south with requested drinking fountains.

Important details include the clarification about the breakroom access and the proposed changes to the restroom and IT closet locations. The email does not specify any concrete deadlines or numbers but indicates that the team is actively working on the second-floor mezzanine layout. Action items include verifying the need for a wellness room and confirming the restroom and IT closet arrangements, which will require further communication and decision-making among the team members involved in the project.
results.citation: Email from ["Brandon Clymer"], Apr 7, 2026: "Email: Re: Vermillion Rise TI"

results.title: Email: RE: Vermillion Rise TI Coord Call
results.content:
The document is an email chain regarding the coordination call for the Vermillion Rise TI (Tenant Improvement) project. The primary purpose of the correspondence is to confirm the scheduling of a weekly meeting to discuss project progress and deliverables among key stakeholders, including project managers and design directors from various companies involved in the project.

Key topics discussed in the email include the scheduling of the weekly call, the confirmation of participants, and the expectation for deliverables to be prepared for the upcoming meeting. The emails indicate that the call is set for Thursdays at 1 PM, with multiple participants confirming their availability. Notable participants include Bob Wright (Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group), Clay Robinson (Mechanical Engineer at Enviro-Max), Jerome Daksiewicz (Director of Design at DKGR), and Thomas Ryan (also from Enviro-Max).

Important details include the date of the upcoming meeting, which is set for April 9, 2026, and the emphasis on having deliverables ready for discussion. The email chain also highlights the collaborative nature of the project, with multiple stakeholders involved and the necessity of communication to ensure project timelines are met. Action items include ensuring all participants prepare their respective deliverables for the meeting and confirming attendance, as indicated by Bob Wright's request for everyone to be ready for the Thursday meeting.
results.citation: Email from ["Thomas Ryan"], Apr 7, 2026: "Email: RE: Vermillion Rise TI Coord Call"

results.title: Email: Re: 2026-018 Vermillion Rise TI

The email chain involves multiple parties, including Jerome Daksiewicz, Bob Wright, and Noah Porter, and serves to confirm the sharing of project files and access to the Revit model among team members. The primary purpose of this communication is to ensure that all relevant disciplines, particularly the Mechanical team, have the necessary resources and information to proceed with the project.

Key topics discussed in the email include the addition of Clay Robinson to the Bim360 project for access to the Revit model and the confirmation of sharing base plans derived from CAD files. Noah Porter initiated the conversation by providing the base plans and ensuring that they were disseminated to the appropriate team members, including Bob Wright and Grant Cloud. Jerome Daksiewicz later confirmed that Clay would receive the necessary PDF reference files to aid in his work.

Important details include the date of the email, April 3, 2026, and the specific email addresses of the involved parties, which highlight the collaborative nature of the project. The communication emphasizes the need for coordination among team members to ensure that all disciplines are aligned and have access to the required documentation.

Action items include ensuring that Clay Robinson receives the PDF reference files and confirming that all disciplines, including the Mechanical team, have access to the shared resources. This indicates a proactive approach to project management, ensuring that all team members are equipped with the necessary tools to contribute effectively to the Vermillion Rise TI project.

results.title: Email: Fw: Vermillion Rise compactor
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the selection of a compactor for the Vermillion Rise project, primarily between Bob Wright, a Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group, and Mark Ferderer from Ferdimation. The purpose of this email chain is to discuss the specifications and requirements for the compactor needed for the project, particularly in light of building restrictions that necessitate a stationary unit with a chute feed from inside.

Key topics in the email include the need for a specific model of compactor that meets the project’s requirements. Bob Wright initially inquires if Mark has a preferred model number for the compactor, emphasizing the importance of selecting the right equipment. Mark responds by indicating that he has not yet chosen a model due to uncertainty about the type required, given the building restrictions. He shares two potential configurations he has envisioned, suggesting that one configuration may be more suitable than the other but seeks Bob's input on the matter.

Important details include the date of the correspondence, April 9, 2026, and the emphasis on the compactor needing to be stationary with a chute feed. Bob Wright's contact information is also provided, indicating his role and locations of the Alleato Group offices in St. Petersburg, FL, and Indianapolis, IN.

results.title: Email: RE: TAG 11/6/25 Vermillion Rise - Code Review
results.content:
The document is an email thread regarding the code review for the Vermillion Rise project, dated January 22, 2026. It involves multiple stakeholders from the Alleato Group, including Andrew Harpenau, AJ Taylor, and Brandon Clymer, discussing equipment specifications, pricing, and project updates. The primary purpose of this correspondence is to clarify details related to the project's equipment needs and costs, particularly in light of future expansions in the TCCA area.

Key topics discussed include the equipment numbers and quotes from Enviro-Max, with specific attention to a previously mentioned figure of $895,000, which AJ Taylor inquires about to confirm whether it includes future expansion considerations. The email chain also references the need for updated architectural plans following a board decision to reduce the building size by half. The communication highlights the urgency of obtaining updated equipment information to share with electricians for pricing purposes.

Important details include the confirmation of three units required for the project—two for production and one for the remainder area. The email thread indicates that updated pricing and equipment selections were to be finalized shortly, with a request for a formal order revision to be sent to Aaon. Action items include ensuring that all stakeholders have the necessary equipment information and confirming the final project approvals and architectural updates, which are expected to be received by the end of the week.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Harpenau"], Jan 21, 2026: "Email: RE: TAG 11/6/25 Vermillion Rise - Code Review"

results.title: Email: Re: Vermillion Rise TI
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the Vermillion Rise Tenant Improvement (TI) project, primarily involving discussions about design and layout changes. The email is dated April 8, 2026, and is sent by Brandon Clymer, CEO of Alleato Group, to Jerome Daksiewicz and Bob Wright. The communication reflects ongoing project management and design considerations as the team works on the specifications for the tenant improvements.

Key topics discussed in the email include the addition of a vestibule at the main entrance next to Conference Room 37, the potential installation of a restroom for the office at IT 40, and the relocation of the IT closet to the upstairs area. Jerome Daksiewicz also raises questions about the need for a wellness room, which was included in a previous design version, and the access to the breakroom from the corridor rather than from the work floor. Additionally, there is a mention of a second corridor to the south with requested drinking fountains.

Important details include the clarification about the breakroom access and the proposed changes to the restroom and IT closet locations. The email does not specify any concrete deadlines or numbers but indicates that the team is actively working on the second-floor mezzanine layout. Action items include verifying the need for a wellness room and confirming the restroom and IT closet arrangements, which will require further communication and decision-making among the team members involved in the project.
results.citation: Email from ["Brandon Clymer"], Apr 7, 2026: "Email: Re: Vermillion Rise TI"

results.title: Email: Fw: Vermillion Rise compactor
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the selection of a compactor for the Vermillion Rise project, primarily between Bob Wright, a Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group, and Mark Ferderer from Ferdimation. The purpose of this email chain is to discuss the specifications and requirements for the compactor needed for the project, particularly in light of building restrictions that necessitate a stationary unit with a chute feed from inside.

Key topics in the email include the need for a specific model of compactor that meets the project’s requirements. Bob Wright initially inquires if Mark has a preferred model number for the compactor, emphasizing the importance of selecting the right equipment. Mark responds by indicating that he has not yet chosen a model due to uncertainty about the type required, given the building restrictions. He shares two potential configurations he has envisioned, suggesting that one configuration may be more suitable than the other but seeks Bob's input on the matter.

Important details include the date of the correspondence, April 9, 2026, and the emphasis on the compactor needing to be stationary with a chute feed. Bob Wright's contact information is also provided, indicating his role and locations of the Alleato Group offices in St. Petersburg, FL, and Indianapolis, IN. 

The next steps involve Mark providing further details on the configurations he envisions and potentially identifying a model number for the compactor that aligns with the project’s needs. Bob is awaiting Mark's thoughts on the proposed configurations to move forward with the selection process.
results.citation: Email from ["Bob  Wright"], Apr 8, 2026: "Email: Fw: Vermillion Rise compactor"

results.title: Email: RE: Vermillion Rise TI Coord Call
results.content:
The document is an email chain regarding the coordination call for the Vermillion Rise TI (Tenant Improvement) project. The primary purpose of the correspondence is to confirm the scheduling of a weekly meeting to discuss project progress and deliverables among key stakeholders, including project managers and design directors from various companies involved in the project.

Key topics discussed in the email include the scheduling of the weekly call, the confirmation of participants, and the expectation for deliverables to be prepared for the upcoming meeting. The emails indicate that the call is set for Thursdays at 1 PM, with multiple participants confirming their availability. Notable participants include Bob Wright (Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group), Clay Robinson (Mechanical Engineer at Enviro-Max), Jerome Daksiewicz (Director of Design at DKGR), and Thomas Ryan (also from Enviro-Max).

Important details include the date of the upcoming meeting, which is set for April 9, 2026, and the emphasis on having deliverables ready for discussion. The email chain also highlights the collaborative nature of the project, with multiple stakeholders involved and the necessity of communication to ensure project timelines are met. Action items include ensuring all participants prepare their respective deliverables for the meeting and confirming attendance, as indicated by Bob Wright's request for everyone to be ready for the Thursday meeting.
results.citation: Email from ["Thomas Ryan"], Apr 7, 2026: "Email: RE: Vermillion Rise TI Coord Call"

results.title: Email: Re: 2026-018 Vermillion Rise TI
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the Vermillion Rise TI project, specifically referenced as 2026-018. The email chain involves multiple parties, including Bob Wright, Senior Project Manager at Alleato Group, and Jerome Daksiewicz, Director of Design at DKGR. The purpose of the communication is to confirm the sharing of project files and access to design resources among team members involved in the project.

Key topics discussed in the email include the sharing of base plans received from CAD files and ensuring that all relevant disciplines, particularly the Mechanical team, have access to necessary project documents. Bob Wright requests confirmation from Noah Porter, an Architectural Graduate, that the plans have been distributed to all involved parties, including Clay Robinson from Enviro-Max. Jerome Daksiewicz confirms that Clay has been added to the Bim360 project for access to the Revit model and will receive the PDF file for reference.

Important details include the date of the emails, which are dated April 2 and April 3, 2026. Contact information for the involved parties is also provided, including phone numbers and addresses for their respective offices in St. Petersburg, FL, and Indianapolis, IN. The action items include confirming the distribution of plans to all disciplines and ensuring that Clay Robinson has the necessary access to the project files. The communication emphasizes collaboration and the importance of keeping all team members informed and equipped with the right resources for the project.
results.citation: Email from ["Bob  Wright"], Apr 2, 2026: "Email: Re: 2026-018 Vermillion Rise TI"

project.name: Vermillion Rise Warehouse
project.summary: The Vermillion Rise project involves the construction of a new precast building designated for pool chemical storage and manufacturing. The facility is planned to be approximately 232 feet by 452 feet, with 5,000 square feet allocated for office space accommodating 32 employees. Key elements of the project include downsizing the facility from its original plan by 60,000 square feet to reduce costs and accommodate future expansions. The project scope includes detailed architectural, structural, civil, plumbing, and electrical designs to meet the H3 occupancy requirements, focusing on safety and operational efficiency...

openRFIs.subject: Specified fire rating for north wall assembly
Clarification on Footing Depth for Building A

recentMeetings.title: Hillsdale Holdings // Alleato Weekly
recentMeetings.keyPoints: The team discussed vital design aspects and demolition strategies to ensure operational efficiency and cost management. Significant points included retaining existing roof drains to avoid a costly rebuild estimated at $500,000, while ensuring controlled risk. The air recovery unit will be dismantled and reused elsewhere, minimizing new equipment costs. Gas piping's location was clarified, with controlled access to the site for verification. Decisions on HVAC unit sizes aimed to optimize current equipment despite an increase in building volume, with air changes adjusted accordingly. Moreover, plans for electrical service upgrades and compactor placements were evaluated, and compliance with sprinkler standards was confirmed to streamline design approval. The next steps include site verificat
recentMeetings.participants: jdawson@alleatogroup.com, bclymer@alleatogroup.com, gcloud@alleatogroup.com, mferderer@ferdimation.com, bclymer@alleatogroup.com, gcloud@alleatogroup.com, mferderer@ferdimation.com

recentMeetings.title: Vermillion Rise Existing building discussions
recentMeetings.keyPoints: The team discussed adjustments to the HVAC layout for a newly assigned 50,000 sq ft building, which is set to improve duct routing and production area flexibility. All HVAC units will be placed on the ground level in specified corners to align with future expansions, while ductwork will be positioned outside to avoid obstruction by tall racking. Structural engineering collaboration is crucial for determining wall penetrations, ensuring compliance with precast panel constraints. Pricing updates are anticipated soon, with a separation between office and warehouse costs to enhance budget clarity. The team aims to accommodate urgent client requests for responsive pricing while continuing layout refinements to minimize costs and maintain the project schedule.

recentMeetings.participants: clay.robinson@enviro-max.net, bclymer@alleatogroup.com, gcloud@alleatogroup.com, andrewh@acereps.com, thomas.ryan@enviro-max.net, bclymer@alleatogroup.com, gcloud@alleatogroup.com, andrewh@acereps.com, thomas.ryan@enviro-max.net

recentMeetings.title: Vermillion design weekly call
recentMeetings.keyPoints: The team discussed critical updates regarding HVAC and plumbing coordination ahead of slab pouring, emphasizing the decision to route the main water line underground to prevent hanging pipes and facilitate maintenance. A request for a plumbing valve addition near the sump was acknowledged for enhancing fluid control. HVAC coordination with electrical systems was also highlighted, with revised drawings due next Wednesday. Structural plan updates were necessary to align column placements with the second-floor build-out, which requires timely sharing to prevent rework. Detailed electrical drawings are expected early next week for owner review, including specific drop locations. Lastly, the team agreed to consolidate questions for clearer communication with the owner's representative during an

recentMeetings.title: Vermillion design weekly call
recentMeetings.keyPoints: The team reviewed the project design progress, confirming they are on track to meet the May 15 submission deadline. Key focuses included finalizing architectural finishes and interior elevations, with Jerome ensuring schedules align with the main plan. Mechanical drawings are mostly complete, requiring minor adjustments for fan and door placements, while plumbing layout needs just one remaining connection.
